title: Aster eatonii
intro: Aster eatonii, also known as Eaton's aster, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and promote wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Aster eatonii include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aster eatonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Aster eatonii is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Aster, to which Aster eatonii belongs, consists of flowering plants native to the Northern Hemisphere. The species Aster eatonii is further classified into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Aster eatonii is also known as Eaton's aster.
uses: Aster eatonii is not a commonly documented species, but some information suggests that related aster species have been used in traditional medicine.
